data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: YouTube employs a microservices architecture, allowing for scalable and independent deployment of service components.
data.technical_spec: Each service component handles a specific function like video playback, user data, comments section, etc.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Utilizes CDNs to deliver media content efficiently and reduce latency worldwide.
data.technical_spec: High-speed delivery through caching nodes spread across global data centers.

data.title: Data Redundancy and Replication
data.description: Ensures data availability through redundant storage and replication strategies.
data.technical_spec: Uses distributed storage systems for video and metadata.

data.title: API-Driven Infrastructure
data.description: Facilitates third-party integrations and internal operations with a robust API infrastructure.
data.technical_spec: RESTful and gRPC APIs for various YouTube services.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Distributes user requests evenly across multiple servers to ensure efficient processing and availability.
data.technical_spec: Employs hardware and software load balancers like Google Front End (GFE).
